stemHtml: Two pilots are preparing for a flight in a multi-crew aircraft. They plan to fly to a location where the approach and landing will need to be carried out under low-visibility conditions. To execute the approach procedure...

options.a: The Commander and the Co-Pilot must hold valid instrument ratings and have completed at least one approach to CAT I minima within the last 365 days.
options.b: Both pilots must hold valid instrument ratings but only the Commander must have completed at least one approach to CAT I minima within the previous 365 days.
options.c: The Commander and the Co-Pilot must hold valid instrument ratings and be trained, checked and current on the applicable low-visibility operations.
options.d: Both pilots must hold valid instrument ratings but only the Commander must have completed the Operator's LVO training and checking programme.
